rationale: The citing paper’s contribution and prior-work framing are about Web visit duration/depth and triple-heterogeneity random-effects modeling, while the cited paper is about translating sales-promotion elasticity decomposition into net unit-sales brand-switching effects in CPG scanner data. The notes show no direct topical or methodological linkage (the citing paper names other foundational work instead), and the only match evidence is a single DOI reference entry with no contextual citation text. This looks most like a peripheral/background mention rather than substantive use.
